name: Duck Miramonte
recipeIngredient: 2 cups duck stock or jellied demi-glaze (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat your oven to 450°F.
2. Bone the duck, reserving any unused parts for the stock. Trim the breasts, salt and pepper well. Add the trimmings to the other bones and reserved for stock.
3. In a sauté pan, heat the butter over medium heat. Place the breasts in the pan, skin side down, to melt the fat between the skin and the meat. Cook for about one or two minutes. Increase the heat to high, and brown lightly. Place the pan of breasts in the oven and cook for 8 minutes.
4. For the Wine Sauce:
a. In a copper pan, melt 6 ounces of butter and add the shallots and peppercorns. Cook briefly (1 to 2 minutes).
b. Add Cepes mushrooms and Cabernet Sauvignon. Reduce slightly.
c. Add duck stock or demi-glace and tomatoes, if desired. Cook over medium heat for 20-30 minutes.
d. Add butter if desired, whip, and reserve.
5. For the Bone Marrow:
a. Fill a saucepan with water, add a touch of salt, then bring the salted water to a boil. Add marrow and cook for about 5 minutes. Reserve.
6. For the Spinach:
a. Blanch the spinach in hot, salted water, then press slightly to dry.
b. In a sauté pan, heat the butter until brown (beurre rosée - hazelnut in color). Add spinach, nutmeg, salt and pepper. Cook for about 20 seconds (spinach should have just started to wilt).
7. To serve:
a. Spoon the wine sauce into a pool on the serving plate.
b. Place bone marrow in the sauce.
c. Trim any skin from the duck breasts and place them skin side down on a cutting surface. Slice very thin.
d. Fan the duck slices out on the serving plate over the sauce, garnish with watercress and spinach in a side dish.
